content.title: Asian Stocks Shrug Off Tariff Jitters
content.summary: Asian stocks rose broadly, with South Korea and Taiwan setting records, despite renewed trade uncertainty.  Hong Kong’s Hang Seng Index rose 2.5%.   South Korea’s Kospi composite closed at a record high, as did its biggest component, Samsung Electronics.

content.title: Indian Stocks Advance After U.S. Agrees to Slash Tariffs
content.summary: Indian stocks jumped after President Trump agreed to slash tariffs on the country. The U.S. is lowering levies on Indian goods to 18% after the two nations struck a trade pact and Trump said New Delhi, led by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, agreed to stop buying Russian oil.

content.title: EU-India Trade Deal by the Numbers
content.summary: That's the annual saving equivalent the European Union expects as a result of its newly struck trade deal with India.  The EU says the pact is set to eliminate or reduce tariffs for more than 90% of its goods.  It is the latest example of U.S. trading partners seeking to curb their reliance on America by expanding ties with other markets.

content.title: Investors remain 'cautious' of crypto's bear market rally
content.description:
Cryptocurrency bitcoin (BTC-USD) hovers above $74,000 per token on Tuesday as it continues to hold above its $70,000 benchmark this week.

Yahoo Finance Senior Markets Reporter Ines Ferré explains Wall Street's caution as investors remain wary that crypto's bear market may not be over just yet.
content.summary: Cryptocurrency bitcoin (BTC-USD) hovers above $74,000 per token on Tuesday as it continues to hold above its $70,000 benchmark this week. Yahoo Finance Senior Markets Reporter Ines Ferré explains Wall Street's caution as investors remain wary that crypto's bear market may not be over just yet.

Big Banks still moving at 'full throttle' as earnings roll out
content.description:
Earnings season has kicked off for major banks after JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), and Citigroup (C) all reported results that beat quarterly estimates on Tuesday.

Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland and Kelly Intelligence founder Kevin Kelly weigh in on the state of the banking industry, especially as more financial institutions adopt crypto and lean heavier into M&A.

content.title: India's TCS tops estimates, says new AI models did not dent services demand
content.summary: Tata Consultancy Services reported better-than-expected quarterly results on Thursday and said that new artificial intelligence models and tools in ‌the market did not hurt demand for its offerings.  TCS, which also provides AI services to its clients, said ‌its annualised AI revenue crossed $2.3 billion ⁠in the fourth quarter, driven by accelerated deployments across industries, up from $1.8 billion in the third quarter.  "It is pretty ⁠minuscule," said Anshul Jethi, analyst at LKP Securities, comparing it to the size at which TCS is currently operating right now and its future AI plans.

content.title: Korean AI chip startup DEEPX, Hyundai work on robots powered by generative AI
content.summary: South Korean AI chip startup DEEPX will expand its partnership with Hyundai Motor Group to develop a computing platform for generative ‌AI robots using its second generation of low-power chips, its top executive said, as ‌it gets set for an IPO.  DEEPX is in talks with the government and investors to raise more than 600 ​billion won ($408 million) in an ongoing funding round, in the run-up to a potential IPO in South Korea, DEEPX CEO Lokwon Kim said.  The company, which began producing chips late last year, is one of a number of South Korean startups riding on Seoul's ambitions to nurture artificial intelligence champions and ‌make the country an AI leader.

content.title: Apple Lawsuit Puts Apple Pay Controls And Services Valuation In Focus
content.summary: Apple has been named as a defendant in a wide reaching lawsuit alleging failure to prevent high frequency gambling transactions processed via Apple Pay. The case centers on claims that Apple, alongside other financial and e commerce firms, enabled hundreds of rapid gambling payments tied to an allegedly fraudulent gaming operator. Plaintiffs are seeking substantial damages and injunctive relief, raising questions about Apple Pay’s consumer protection controls and regulatory compliance. For...

content.title: Microsoft’s 3,200 Acres Data Center Land Purchase—and the Utility Stock That Benefits
content.summary: Black Hills Corp.  shares rose on Tuesday after  Microsoft  announced plans to purchase approximately 3,200 acres in Cheyenne, Wyo., to develop a data center.  The project will add to Microsoft’s footprint in the state, as hyperscalers such as  Meta Platforms  have started to build out data centers in Wyoming.  Microsoft already has 11 data centers in the state, with three more currently being built.
